purpose: Formal check result registry. This records actual result status separately from source extraction and check-target definition.

results.result_summary: Implemented finite pre-bracketing/bracketed-tree consistency checker on selected small tree fixtures. The checker verifies that source-stated delete-bracket and contract-minimal-bracket covers generate an acyclic finite poset with antisymmetric transitive closure. This is partial finite evidence only; it does not prove the full Matryoshka face-lattice anti-isomorphism and is not an all-n theorem.

Implemented finite bracketed-tree cover-rule checker on selected small tree fixtures. The checker verifies the source-stated local move split for cosmohedral/bracketed-tree covers: delete a bracket versus contract a minimal bracket. This is partial finite evidence only; it does not complete the full A/M1 comparison burden and is not an all-n theorem.

Implemented R12 submodular-law source guard. The checker verifies that the cosmohedron grouped-inequality, positive-delta, submodular-compatibility, and delta_full=0 interface claims remain SOURCE_EXTRACTED / NEEDS_FORMALIZATION source-bound material. This is not an SMT/Z3 proof, not a full submodular-law theorem, not Formation Medium substrate evidence, not a physical settling law, and not engineering readiness.

Implemented R13 equality-layer source guard. The checker verifies that cosmohedron inequality-layer and face-lattice/Matryoshka-poset claims remain SOURCE_EXTRACTED / NEEDS_FORMALIZATION source-bound inputs for a later intrinsic/native vs degenerative/saturation equality-classification model. This is not a typed predicate model, not a Lean/Z3 proof, not an intrinsic-vs-degenerative theorem, not Formation Medium substrate evidence, not a physical settling law, and not engineering readiness.

Implemented finite noncrossing-chord face-poset checker for n=4..8. Every cover relation is strict inclusion by exactly one added noncrossing diagonal under rank(face)=number_of_diagonals; max rank matches n-3 for each checked n. This checks the declared finite baseline only, not a Lean theorem.

Implemented finite global-sign-normalized sign-flip poset checker for n=3..9. Sign words are encoded by adjacent sign-change subsets; covers add exactly one adjacent sign-change edge; state and cover counts match the finite binary model. This is partial finite evidence for the declared sign-vector model only, not a full amplituhedron theorem.

Implemented surfaceology u-equation source guard. The checker verifies that surfaceology u-variable, curve-integral, all-multiplicity, scaffolded-gluon reuse, and colored-Yukawa extension claims remain SOURCE_EXTRACTED / NEEDS_FORMALIZATION source-bound interface material. This is not a finite curve-intersection model, not a proof of the u-equation, not Formation Medium substrate evidence, not a physical settling law, and not engineering readiness.

Implemented surfaceology binary-boundary source guard. The checker verifies that surfaceology positive-region, u-variable, and binary-geometry claims remain SOURCE_EXTRACTED / NEEDS_FORMALIZATION source-bound interface material. This is not a positive-region solver, not a binary-boundary theorem, not Formation Medium substrate evidence, not a physical settling law, and not engineering readiness.

Implemented the first tiny finite surfaceology fixture check. The checker validates the two-curve crossing fixture with exact rational arithmetic: symmetric intersection exponents derive u_a + u_b - 1 = 0 and u_b + u_a - 1 = 0; three sample points satisfy the equations inside 0 <= u <= 1; and boundary samples force the crossing variable to one. This is finite fixture evidence only, not a surfaceology theorem, not a general positive-region solver, not Formation Medium substrate evidence, not a physical settling law, and not engineering readiness.

Implemented finite surfaceology fixture-family check. The checker validates three declared crossing-graph fixtures with exact rational arithmetic, including a two-curve crossing pair, two disconnected crossing pairs, and a three-curve chain whose middle curve has a two-neighbor product equation u_b + u_a * u_c - 1 = 0. This is finite fixture-family evidence only, not a surfaceology theorem, not a general positive-region solver, not Formation Medium substrate evidence, not a physical settling law, and not engineering readiness.

Implemented scaffold/kinematic-shift ledger guard. The checker verifies that hidden-zero, scalar-scaffolded-gluon shift, and W_e/transmutation claims remain SOURCE_EXTRACTED / NEEDS_FORMALIZATION bridge material, while blocking promotion to Yang-Mills derivation, realization-class equivalence, Formation Medium substrate evidence, physical settling law, or engineering readiness.

Implemented same-boundary/different-bulk ledger guard. The checker verifies that EXT-AMP-PRIME-001 and EXT-WILSON-POS-2024-001 remain source-extracted cautions, and that Formation Medium artifacts do not promote shared boundary structure, shared logarithmic form, sign behavior, or bootstrap success into bulk equivalence, substrate realization, engineering readiness, or physical settling-law evidence.

Implemented representation-comparator ledger guard for momentum amplituhedron and loop momentum amplituhedron source records. The checker verifies that spinor-helicity/tree and loop-momentum positive-geometry claims remain SOURCE_EXTRACTED / NEEDS_FORMALIZATION representation-comparator entries, while blocking promotion to equality of geometric realization class, bulk equivalence, Formation Medium substrate equivalence, or engineering readiness without a separate map/boundary/bulk proof.

Source-extracted claims have source anchors, statuses, and linked formal check targets; every formal check target links back to extracted claims; the 16-paper source manifest and PDF binding registry are present and must be fully covered by extracted claims and repo PDF pointers.

Implemented POS_GEOM_2017 canonical-boundary recursion source guard. The checker verifies that the source text and extracted claims contain canonical-form uniqueness, boundary-only logarithmic singularity, and residue-on-boundary recursion language. This is source-coverage discipline only: not a formal residue theorem, not an all-positive-geometries theorem, not S2* checked status, and not substrate or settling-law evidence.

Implemented ledger source-coverage guard for S2* readout support. The checker verifies that POS_GEOM_2017, scattering-forms, and Into-the-Amplituhedron source-extracted claims remain SOURCE_EXTRACTED / NEEDS_FORMALIZATION, that generated Formation Medium artifacts acknowledge POS_GEOM_2017 source intake, and that no checked/substrate/settling-law promotion is inferred. This is source-coverage discipline only, not proof or physical realization evidence.

Implemented POS_GEOM_2017 triangulation/push-forward source guard. The checker verifies that the source text and extracted claim contain triangulation and push-forward language as methods for constructing canonical forms of positive geometries, while blocking promotion to Formation Medium dynamics, substrate realization, physical settling law, or computation-replacement evidence.

Implemented Formation Medium readiness ledger guard. The checker verifies that source/finite-check progress is separated from formal theorem, physical substrate, native settling-law, engineering prototype, product-readiness, and capital-readiness claims. This is a scope-control validation artifact only, not a proof, not a substrate selection, not engineering readiness, and not a product-readiness claim.

Implemented Formation Medium technical roadmap guard. The checker verifies that the roadmap is a bounded T0/T1/T2 execution queue aligned with readiness limits, with explicit do-now, capital-dependent, and do-not-do-yet boundaries. This is not a proof, not a substrate selection, not engineering readiness, not capital readiness, and not a pitch deck.

non_promotion_rule: Only PASS results from implemented mathematical checkers may promote a row within the declared finite scope. DEFINED_NOT_RUN is not evidence for the mathematical claim. PASS_PARTIAL_FINITE_CHECK is finite evidence within its declared scope, not theorem status. PASS_SOURCE_COVERAGE is source-coverage discipline only; it is not theorem status, not checked S2*, and not substrate or settling-law evidence.
